paper_title: Miocene Climate Simulations and Data-Model Comparison

methods.description: Setup of the Miocene climate model simulations using AWI-ESM2.1 to study the effects of different CO2 concentrations and boundary conditions on climate.

steps.description: Prescribe an atmospheric CO2 concentration of 450 ppm for the reference Miocene simulation (MIO_450).
steps.searchable_summary: set atmospheric CO2 concentration to 450 ppm in climate model

steps.description: Implement high-resolution paleobathymetry and paleotopography of the Middle Miocene.
steps.searchable_summary: apply paleobathymetry and paleotopography to climate model

steps.description: Create an ocean mesh using the paleobathymetry and paleotopography.
steps.searchable_summary: generate ocean mesh from paleobathymetry and paleotopography

steps.description: Run FESOM2 in standalone mode for around 300 model years to create an initial spin-up of the ocean state.
steps.searchable_summary: run FESOM2 standalone for ocean state spin-up

steps.description: Initialize the coupled atmosphere-ocean model from the standalone ocean spin-up and integrate for 1,000 model years.
steps.searchable_summary: initialize coupled model from ocean spin-up and integrate

steps.description: Perform additional simulations (MIO_280, MIO_720, PI_450) to analyze effects of CO2 and boundary conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: run additional climate simulations with varying CO2 and conditions

steps.description: Utilize factor separation analysis to analyze the effect of boundary conditions and their interactions.
steps.searchable_summary: perform factor separation analysis on simulation results

steps.description: Employ zero-dimensional and one-dimensional energy balance models to investigate mechanisms governing global surface temperature changes.
steps.searchable_summary: use energy balance models to study temperature change mechanisms

methods.description: Comparison of simulated annual-mean surface temperatures with proxy data estimates for the Miocene period.

steps.description: Compare simulated annual-mean surface temperatures of land and ocean against available proxy data estimates.
steps.searchable_summary: compare simulated temperatures with proxy data

steps.description: Analyze the meridional temperature gradient in simulations and compare with proxy data.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and compare meridional temperature gradient

steps.description: Calculate correlation coefficients and RMSE between simulations and proxy data.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate correlation and RMSE for model validation

steps.description: Evaluate model performance in capturing low to mid-latitude temperature distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: evaluate model performance for latitude temperature distribution
